# services.btrbk

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.btrbk`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.btrbk.extraPackages` | `list of package` | Extra packages for btrbk, like compression utilities for `stream_compress`. **Note**: This option will get deprecated in future releases. Required compression programs will get automatically provided to btrbk depending on configured compression method in `services.btrbk.instances.<name>.settings` option. |
| `services.btrbk.instances` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Set of btrbk instances. The instance named `btrbk` is the default one. |
| `services.btrbk.instances.<name>.onCalendar` | `null or string` | How often this btrbk instance is started. See {manpage}`systemd.time(7)` for more information about the format. Setting it to null disables the timer, thus this instance can only be started manually. |
| `services.btrbk.instances.<name>.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (string or instances of this type recursively)` | configuration options for btrbk. Nested attrsets translate to subsections. |
| `services.btrbk.instances.<name>.settings.stream_compress` | `one of "gzip", "pigz", "bzip2", "pbzip2", "bzip3", "xz", "lzo", "lz4", "zstd", "no"` | Compress the btrfs send stream before transferring it from/to remote locations using a compression command. |
| `services.btrbk.instances.<name>.snapshotOnly` | `boolean` | Whether to run in snapshot only mode. This skips backup creation and deletion steps. Useful when you want to manually backup to an external drive that might not always be connected. Use `btrbk -c /path/to/conf resume` to trigger manual backups. More examples [here](https://github.com/digint/btrbk#example-backups-to-usb-disk). See also `snapshot` subcommand in {manpage}`btrbk(1)`. |
| `services.btrbk.ioSchedulingClass` | `one of "idle", "best-effort", "realtime"` | IO scheduling class for btrbk (see {manpage}`ionice(1)` for a quick description). Applies to local instances, and remote ones connecting by ssh if set to idle. |
| `services.btrbk.niceness` | `integer between -20 and 19 (both inclusive)` | Niceness for local instances of btrbk. Also applies to remote ones connecting via ssh when positive. |
| `services.btrbk.sshAccess` | `list of (submodule)` | SSH keys that should be able to make or push snapshots on this system remotely with btrbk |
| `services.btrbk.sshAccess.*.extraArgs` | `list of string` | Additional arguments to pass to ssh_filter_btrbk |
| `services.btrbk.sshAccess.*.key` | `string` | SSH public key allowed to login as user `btrbk` to run remote backups. |
| `services.btrbk.sshAccess.*.roles` | `list of (one of "info", "source", "target", "delete", "snapshot", "send", "receive")` | What actions can be performed with this SSH key. See ssh_filter_btrbk(1) for details |
